commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=357abf14533bb73928301002f9c1cf3b4d3910ef)
commands.review_summary:
The PR disables eager closed-menu preparation by default, adds DEBUG-only menu refresh test hooks, updates related menu/settings tests, and changes the default OpenAI Web battery saver setting to enabled.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: an absent `openAIWebBatterySaverEnabled` key would now read true, and the OpenAI Web refresh gate skips non-forced regular refreshes when that value is true.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor and maintainer handling rather than an automated repair because the code blocker is tied to upgrade semantics and the missing after-fix proof must come from a real setup.

target.title: Reduce idle menu power usage
target.body:
## Summary
- Stop eager prewarming for closed menus by default so idle invalidations do not spend SwiftUI layout work on menu cards.
- Keep the closed-menu rebuild path covered behind the existing DEBUG-only test hooks, and update the main behavior test to refresh stale content on next open.
- Default OpenAI Web battery saver to enabled for new settings.

## Background
A local CPU sample of the running menu bar app showed idle CPU dominated by `StatusItemController.rebuildClosedMenuIfNeeded(_:)` -> `populateMenu(_:provider:)` -> SwiftUI `NSHostingController.sizeThatFits(in:)`, while the menu was closed. WebKit helper processes were not the hot path in that sample.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `.build/lint-tools/bin/swiftformat --lint .`
- `swift build --product CodexBarCLI`

## Local limitations
- `swift test --filter 'StatusMenuTests|SettingsStoreTests|ProviderSettingsDescriptorTests'` and `swift build --target CodexBar` were attempted locally, but this machine only has Command Line Tools selected and no full Xcode install. The GUI build fails before this change on missing SwiftUI macro plugins (`PreviewsMacros` / `SwiftUIMacros`).
- `make check` ran SwiftFormat successfully, then SwiftLint failed locally because SourceKitten could not load `sourcekitdInProc` under the current CLT-only setup.
target.author: nicolas0315
